question: Which states have the worst health burden?
How unequal is healthcare access across districts (the care lottery)?
Which districts have zero facilities in this dataset?
Which facilities claim advanced care they can't back up?
Does facility supply follow health need across India?
Where are India's medical deserts (high need, low facility coverage)?

content:
<!-- Audience: Genie LLM — system instructions for the India Healthcare Access space (Virtue Foundation, DAIS-for-Good 2026, Medical Desert Planner) -->

# India Healthcare Access — Genie instructions

## Tables (3, all under `databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset`)
- `facilities` — SUPPLY. ~10k web-scraped health facilities (lat/lon, type, operator, self-reported specialties/equipment/capability). One row per facility.
- `nfhs_5_district_health_indicators` — DEMAND/NEED. 706 rows, one per district, ~107 NFHS-5 (2019-21) health indicators. Says NOTHING about supply.
- `india_post_pincode_directory` — GEO CROSSWALK. PIN -> district -> state. Post-office grain (8.5 rows/PIN), NOT unique.

## Honesty contract (ALWAYS apply — this is scored)
1. `facilities` is a ~10k web-discovered SAMPLE, NOT a census (India's real registry is 47k+). Frame every facility count / desert / gap as in-dataset COVERAGE, never absolute supply. A zero-facility district = zero IN THIS DATASET (a coverage/outreach gap), not "no care exists".
2. NO per-capita anywhere — there is no population column in any table.
3. Every facility attribute (specialties, equipment, capability, numberDoctors, capacity) is self-reported SCRAPED text — a CLAIM, never a verified fact.
4. A "medical desert" claim REQUIRES joining high NFHS burden (demand) to LOW in-dataset facility coverage (supply). NFHS alone = need only.

## Universal cleaning rules
- facilities: ALWAYS filter `address_country='India'` (88 of 10,088 rows are CSV-misparse junk -> leaves 10,000).
- facilities: missing values are the literal STRING 'null' (also 'none'/'NA'/''), NOT SQL NULL. Strip these before any presence/fill-rate/aggregation. `numberDoctors` looks 99.7% populated but is real for only 36%.
- facilities: `unique_id` is NOT unique — 11 ids are byte-identical dupes. Use `COUNT(DISTINCT unique_id)` (10,088 -> 9,989) before any facility count.
- facilities numeric-but-STRING cols (`numberDoctors`,`capacity`,`address_zipOrPostcode`): `try_cast(regexp_replace(col,'[()*]','') AS DOUBLE)` and require >0 before treating as evidence.
- facilities `operatorTypeId` has a literal string 'null' (687 rows) distinct from real NULL — exclude before private/public share.
- facilities `organization_type` is the constant 'facility' — never group by it.

## NFHS rules
- 49 of 107 indicator columns are STRING-but-numeric. ALWAYS `try_cast(regexp_replace(col,'[()*]','') AS DOUBLE)`; casting raw fails/zeroes.
- '*' in a cell = SUPPRESSED (sample too small) -> treat as NULL, NEVER 0 (0 fabricates a "perfect" district). 
- '(29.5)' parenthesized = low-confidence estimate (25-49 cases) — usable but flag, never a headline.
- `health_burden_index` is a RELATIVE percentile rank (0=lowest,100=highest burden), not a prevalence.
- Vintage = NFHS-5 2019-21 baseline; never label current-year.

## Normalization (before any rollup/join)
- State: `address_stateOrRegion` is polluted (234 distinct vs 36 real). UPPER/strip + alias map (Tamilnadu->Tamil Nadu, Orissa->Odisha, UP->Uttar Pradesh, Chattisgarh->Chhattisgarh) before state rollups.
- District: normalize UPPER -> trim -> collapse whitespace on BOTH sides of any facility<->NFHS join. 704/706 NFHS district names carry trailing whitespace.
- District name collides across states (Aurangabad = Bihar+Maharashtra, Bijapur, Balrampur, Bilaspur, ...). Key on (normalized state, district), never district name alone (double-counts +85 rows).

## THE join spine (supply <-> demand)
facility `address_zipOrPostcode` (extract 6-digit PIN with `try_cast(regexp_extract(address_zipOrPostcode,'([0-9]{6})',1) AS INT)` — regexp_extract returns '' on no-match and a plain `CAST('' AS INT)` HARD-FAILS, so ALWAYS try_cast) -> `india_post_pincode_directory` deduped to the MODAL (district,statename) per pincode (it fans out 8x) -> normalize -> `nfhs_5_district_health_indicators.district_name`. Coverage: 97.7% PIN parse -> 95.7% PIN->district -> 81.2% match an NFHS district. See the certified example "How do facilities map to NFHS districts".

## Defaults
- "highest burden / worst health" = rank by NFHS indicators (demand). "medical desert / gap" = high burden AND low facility coverage (needs the join). "supply / facilities" = facilities table only.
- Round %s to 1 decimal. Report counts as in-dataset coverage.
- Headline NFHS cols all_w15_49_who_are_anaemic_pct, hh_member_covered_health_insurance_pct, institutional_birth_5y_pct are already DOUBLE. NEVER filter `col <> '*'` (that casts '*' to DOUBLE and FAILS). The universal-safe pattern for ANY NFHS numeric (string OR double): `try_cast(regexp_replace(col,'[()*]','') AS DOUBLE)` in a subquery, then filter `IS NOT NULL`.

question: How do facilities map to NFHS districts (the PIN crosswalk join)?
sql:
WITH pin_modal AS (
  SELECT pincode,
         FIRST(district) OVER (PARTITION BY pincode ORDER BY cnt DESC) AS district,
         FIRST(statename) OVER (PARTITION BY pincode ORDER BY cnt DESC) AS statename
  FROM (SELECT pincode, district, statename, COUNT(*) cnt
        FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.india_post_pincode_directory
        GROUP BY pincode, district, statename)),
fac AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT unique_id,
         try_cast(regexp_extract(address_zipOrPostcode,'([0-9]{6})',1) AS INT) AS pin
  FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.facilities WHERE address_country='India')
SELECT upper(trim(p.district)) AS district_norm, p.statename, COUNT(f.unique_id) AS facilities
FROM fac f LEFT JOIN pin_modal p ON f.pin=p.pincode
GROUP BY upper(trim(p.district)), p.statename
ORDER BY facilities DESC LIMIT 20

question: Which states have the worst health burden (women anaemia as a burden proxy)?
sql: SELECT TRIM(state_ut) AS state, COUNT(*) AS districts, ROUND(AVG(v),1) AS mean_women_anaemia_pct FROM (SELECT state_ut, try_cast(regexp_replace(all_w15_49_who_are_anaemic_pct,'[()*]','') AS DOUBLE) AS v FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.nfhs_5_district_health_indicators) WHERE v IS NOT NULL GROUP BY TRIM(state_ut) HAVING COUNT(*)>=3 ORDER BY mean_women_anaemia_pct DESC

question: Which states have the most facilities (supply concentration)?
sql:
SELECT address_stateOrRegion AS state, COUNT(DISTINCT unique_id) AS facilities,
       ROUND(100.0*COUNT(DISTINCT unique_id)/SUM(COUNT(DISTINCT unique_id)) OVER (),1) AS pct_of_total
FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.facilities
WHERE address_country='India'
GROUP BY address_stateOrRegion
ORDER BY facilities DESC LIMIT 15

question: What facility types and operators are in the dataset?
sql:
SELECT facilityTypeId,
       CASE WHEN operatorTypeId='null' THEN 'unknown' ELSE operatorTypeId END AS operator,
       COUNT(DISTINCT unique_id) AS facilities
FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.facilities
WHERE address_country='India'
GROUP BY facilityTypeId, operator
ORDER BY facilities DESC

question: Which districts have the worst child anaemia (health burden)?
sql:
SELECT district_name, state_ut,
       ROUND(try_cast(regexp_replace(child_6_59m_who_are_anaemic_lt_11_0_g_dl_22_pct,'[()*]','') AS DOUBLE),1) AS child_anaemia_pct
FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.nfhs_5_district_health_indicators
WHERE child_6_59m_who_are_anaemic_lt_11_0_g_dl_22_pct <> '*'
ORDER BY child_anaemia_pct DESC LIMIT 15

question: How many facilities are in the dataset and how clean is it?
sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_rows,
       COUNT_IF(address_country='India') AS india_rows,
       COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN address_country='India' THEN unique_id END) AS distinct_india_facilities
FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.facilities

question: Which districts have zero facilities in the dataset (the coverage gap / medical deserts)?
sql:
WITH pin_modal AS (
  SELECT pincode,
         FIRST(district) OVER (PARTITION BY pincode ORDER BY cnt DESC) AS district
  FROM (SELECT pincode, district, COUNT(*) cnt
        FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.india_post_pincode_directory GROUP BY pincode, district)),
fac AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT unique_id,
         try_cast(regexp_extract(address_zipOrPostcode,'([0-9]{6})',1) AS INT) AS pin
  FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.facilities WHERE address_country='India'),
resolved AS (
  SELECT upper(trim(p.district)) AS district_norm, f.unique_id
  FROM fac f JOIN pin_modal p ON f.pin=p.pincode)
SELECT n.district_name, n.state_ut, COUNT(r.unique_id) AS facilities_in_dataset
FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.nfhs_5_district_health_indicators n
LEFT JOIN resolved r ON upper(trim(n.district_name)) = r.district_norm
GROUP BY n.district_name, n.state_ut
HAVING COUNT(r.unique_id)=0
ORDER BY n.state_ut, n.district_name

question: Which facilities claim advanced care (MRI/ICU/cancer) with no hard proof?
sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT unique_id) AS unverifiable_advanced_claimers
FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.facilities
WHERE address_country='India'
  AND (lower(equipment) RLIKE '(mri|ct scan|icu|cancer|oncolog|cardiac|cathlab|dialysis|nicu|transplant)'
    OR lower(capability) RLIKE '(mri|ct|icu|cancer|cardiac|dialysis|nicu|transplant)')
  AND (numberDoctors='null' OR numberDoctors IS NULL)
  AND (capacity='null' OR capacity IS NULL)

question: How unequal is healthcare access across districts (the care lottery)?
sql:
SELECT 'health_insurance_pct' AS indicator,
       ROUND(MIN(v),1) AS min_district, ROUND(MAX(v),1) AS max_district,
       ROUND(MAX(v)/NULLIF(MIN(v),0),1) AS spread_x
FROM (SELECT try_cast(hh_member_covered_health_insurance_pct AS DOUBLE) AS v
      FROM databricks_virtue_foundation_dataset_dais_2026.virtue_foundation_dataset.nfhs_5_district_health_indicators)
WHERE v IS NOT NULL
